graphdb_rdf.metric_question: Outline the complete legal framework for law enforcement use of real-time remote biometric identification: permitted uses, pre-deployment safeguards and limitations, authorization requirements and authority, and notification and reporting obligations.
graphdb_rdf.metric_answer: The legal framework for the law enforcement use of real-time remote biometric identification under the EU AI Act encompasses various aspects, including permitted uses, pre-deployment safeguards and limitations, authorization requirements, and notification and reporting obligations. Below is an outline based on the relevant entities in the knowledge graph: ### 1. Permitted Uses - **Law Enforcement Purposes**: The use of real-time remote biometric identification systems is primarily permitted for law enforcement activities, such as identifying individuals involved in criminal activities or ensuring public safety in high-risk situations. - [Law Enforcement](https://euaiact.eu/resource/Organization/law_enforcement) - [Use for the Purpose of Law Enforcement](https://euaiact.eu/resource/Activity/use_for_the_purpose_of_law_enforcement) ### 2. Pre-Deployment Safeguards and Limitations - **Assessment of Intrusiveness**: The technology is characterized as particularly intrusive, which necessitates a thorough assessment of its impact on rights and freedoms. - [Particularly Intrusive](https://euaiact.eu/resource/Characteristic/particularly_intrusive) - **Strict Exceptions**: The use of these systems is subject to strict exceptions, meaning they can only be deployed under specific circumstances, such as serious criminal offenses. - [Prohibition and Strict Exceptions for Real-Time Remote Biometric Identification](https://euaiact.eu/resource/Policy/prohibition_and_strict_exceptions_for_real_time_remote_biometric_identification) ### 3. Authorization Requirements and Authority - **Authorization Process**: Law enforcement authorities must obtain authorization before deploying real-time remote biometric identification systems. This includes compliance with national laws compatible with Union law. - [Authorization to Place on the Market or Put into Service AI Systems Without Conformity Assessment](https://euaiact.eu/resource/Policy/authorisation_to_place_on_the_market_or_put_into_service_AI_systems_without_conformity_assessment) - **Relevant Law Enforcement Authority**: Specific authorities are designated to oversee and authorize the use of such technologies. - [Relevant Law Enforcement Authority](https://euaiact.eu/resource/Organization/relevant_law_enforcement_authority) ### 4. Notification and Reporting Obligations - **Reporting Obligations**: Law enforcement agencies using real-time remote biometric identification systems must report on their usage, including the effectiveness and any incidents of misuse or malfunction. - [Data Related to Use of Real-Time Biometric Identification Systems](https://euaiact.eu/resource/Data/data_related_to_use_of_real-time_biometric_identification_systems) - **Monitoring and Compliance**: Continuous monitoring of the systems' performance and compliance with regulations is required to ensure that they are used appropriately and do not infringe on individuals' rights. - [Monitoring Obligation Placed on Deployers of High-Risk AI Systems](https://euaiact.eu/resource/Obligation/monitoring_obligation_placed_on_deployers_of_high-risk_AI_systems) This framework aims to balance the needs of law enforcement with the protection of fundamental rights and freedoms, ensuring that the deployment of such technologies is justified, transparent, and accountable.
